titleStmt.title: Letter from Kerin & Hickman to the Under Secretary, 3 August, 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Kerin & Hickman

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from The Solicitors Kerin & Hickman to the Under Secretary. Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) succeeded Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), who resigned on 3 May 1916 due to his failure to prevent the Easter Rising.
The letter concerns the summons that Kerin & Hickman issued a summons to recover £8.8.1 against a member of Kilrush (Clare) District Council. They are concerned that that it is unlikely in this case that the required two Magistrates will attend the relevant Petty Session. They suggest that, under the circumstances, it would be appropriate for the CSO to allocate a second Resident Magistrate.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5620/13361

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
KERIN & HICKMAN,
Solicitors.
FREDERICH G. KERIN
FRANCIS W. [...]-HICKMAN.
TELEPHONE No. 12. ENNIS. Ennis
Co. Clare. 3rd August 1916. The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle,
Dublin. Sir, We have issued a Summons at suit of W Boughey against Mr John O’Dwyer
of Market Square Kilrush, a member of the Urban District
Council of Kilrush, to recover a sum of £8.6.1 balance
of a larger sum with which Mr O’Dwyer and other members
and officials of the Urban Council were surcharged. This case aroused a considerable amount of public
interest, and we understand that it is extremely unlikely
that the two Magistrates will be in attendance at the Kilrush
Petty Sessions on Monday next, the 7th instant, which is
the day on which the defendant is summoned to appear.
In previous cases of this kind, where any likelihood
arose of a second Magistrate not attending, the Government
ordered a second Resident Magistrate to be in attendance,
and we respectfully submit that a second Resident Magistrate
will in all probability be required on the hearing of this
case. We believe, from information we have received, that
none of the local Magistrates will adjudicate, and the case
requires two Magistrates. The hour of the Petty Sessions at Kilrush is 12.30 We are Sir
Your obedient Servants,
KERIN & HICKMAN
